responsePrompt:
Let’s say a researcher has a correlation of .5 and a slope of .25. If they switched X and Y.

a. What would happen to the correlation?
answer: The correlation would remain the same because it measures the mutual relationship between variables regardless of which one is X or Y.

responsePrompt: b. What would happen to the slope?
answer: The slope would change and become 1.

responsePrompt: Please list as many ways slope and correlation are the same as you can think of.
answer: Both always share the same sign. Both specifically measure the strength of linear relationships. Both the slope and correlation would be zero if there is no linear relationship. Both require to variables (X and Y).

responsePrompt: Please list as many ways slope and correlation are different as you can think of.
answer: Correlation is always between -1 and 1, while slope can be any negative number to positive infinity. Correlation does not have units but slope does. Correlation stays the same if you change the variables (X and Y), whereas slope changes completely when variables or axes are changed.
